question_en: What is a KPI?
answer_en: A key performance indicator: a metric tied to a business goal used to track progress.
answer_es: Indicador clave: métrica ligada a un objetivo de negocio para medir progreso.

question_en: What is a North Star metric?
answer_en: A single high-level metric that captures long-term customer value and guides teams.
answer_es: Métrica única de alto nivel que captura valor a largo plazo y guía equipos.

question_en: What is a funnel?
answer_en: A sequence of steps users go through (view → signup → purchase) used to measure drop-off.
answer_es: Secuencia de pasos (ver → registro → compra) para medir drop-off.

question_en: Why segment metrics?
answer_en: To find which users/contexts drive changes and avoid misleading averages.
answer_es: Para encontrar qué usuarios/contextos explican cambios y evitar promedios engañosos.

question_en: What is retention?
answer_en: The share of users who come back and are active again after a given time window.
answer_es: Proporción de usuarios que regresan y están activos tras una ventana de tiempo.

question_en: What is an A/B test used for in product?
question_es: ¿Para qué sirve un A/B test en producto?
answer_en: To estimate the causal impact of a change on a metric by random assignment.
answer_es: Para estimar impacto causal de un cambio por asignación aleatoria.

question_en: What is a guardrail metric?
answer_en: A metric you monitor to prevent harmful trade-offs while optimizing the primary metric.
answer_es: Métrica para prevenir trade-offs dañinos mientras optimizas la principal.

question_en: First check when a metric suddenly changes?
answer_en: Instrumentation/data quality: tracking, pipeline freshness, schema changes, and dashboards.
answer_es: Calidad/instrumentación: tracking, frescura, cambios de schema y dashboards.

question_en: How do you communicate results to stakeholders?
answer_en: Lead with the decision, then key numbers, uncertainty, assumptions, and recommended next steps.
answer_es: Empieza por la decisión, luego números clave, incertidumbre, supuestos y próximos pasos.

question_en: What is activation?
answer_en: A user reaching the first meaningful value moment (e.g., first project created).
answer_es: Usuario llegando al primer momento de valor (p. ej. primer proyecto).

question_en: Why use cohorts?
answer_en: To separate user groups by start time and track retention/behavior over time.
answer_es: Para separar grupos por fecha de inicio y seguir retención/comportamiento.

question_en: DAU vs MAU?
answer_en: Daily active users vs monthly active users; define “active” consistently.
answer_es: Usuarios activos diarios vs mensuales; define “activo” consistentemente.

question_en: Why does latency matter?
answer_en: It affects user experience, conversion, and retention; slow products leak users.
answer_es: Afecta experiencia, conversión y retención; productos lentos pierden usuarios.

question_en: Leading vs lagging indicators?
answer_en: Leading predict future outcomes; lagging measure results after the fact.
answer_es: Leading predicen futuro; lagging miden resultados después.

question_en: What is an MDE?
answer_en: Minimum detectable effect: the smallest lift your test is powered to detect.
answer_es: Mínimo efecto detectable: el lift mínimo que el test puede detectar.

question_en: Simple way to prioritize ideas?
answer_en: Use an impact/effort framework (ICE/RICE) and sanity-check assumptions.
answer_es: Usa impacto/esfuerzo (ICE/RICE) y valida supuestos.

question_en: A key metric drops 10% WoW. How do you investigate?
answer_en: Validate tracking, segment (geo/device/channel), pinpoint start time, correlate with releases/incidents, form hypotheses, and quantify drivers.
answer_es: Valida tracking, segmenta, ubica inicio, correlaciona con releases/incidentes, hipótesis y cuantifica drivers.

question_en: Signup conversion drops. What do you check first?
question_es: Baja conversión de registro. ¿Qué revisas primero?
answer_en: Instrumentation + step-level funnel, then slices (platform/browser), and recent changes.
answer_es: Instrumentación + funnel por pasos, luego slices y cambios recientes.

question_en: What makes a good North Star metric?
question_es: ¿Qué hace buena a una North Star?
answer_en: Reflects long-term value, is sensitive to product improvements, and is hard to game.
answer_es: Refleja valor a largo plazo, responde a mejoras y es difícil de manipular.

question_en: What makes a good dashboard?
answer_en: Clear questions, stable definitions, few key charts, segments, alerts, and links to drill-down.
answer_es: Preguntas claras, definiciones estables, pocas gráficas clave, segmentos, alertas y drill-down.

question_en: How do you design an A/B test end-to-end?
answer_en: Hypothesis, primary metric + guardrails, sample size/power, randomization unit, duration, logging, analysis plan.
answer_es: Hipótesis, métrica principal+guardrails, muestra/potencia, unidad de randomización, duración, logging y plan.

question_en: How do you pick guardrails?
answer_en: Pick metrics representing user harm or long-term value (refunds, complaints, latency, churn).
answer_es: Elige métricas de daño o valor a largo plazo (reembolsos, quejas, latencia, churn).

question_en: How do you choose segments without p-hacking?
answer_en: Predefine key segments, limit the number, correct for multiple testing, and validate on holdout.
answer_es: Predefine segmentos, limita cantidad, corrige múltiples pruebas y valida en holdout.

question_en: What do you do when metrics disagree?
answer_en: Check definitions, pick a hierarchy (primary/guardrails), quantify trade-offs, and align with goals.
answer_es: Revisa definiciones, jerarquía, cuantifica trade-offs y alinea con objetivos.

question_en: Cohort retention vs rolling retention?
answer_en: Cohort: return on exact day/week. Rolling: return on or after a day (easier to interpret for long horizons).
answer_es: Cohorte: regreso en día exacto. Rolling: regreso en o después (más interpretable a largo plazo).

question_en: How do you account for seasonality in metrics?
answer_en: Compare to prior periods (WoW/YoY), use control charts/baselines, and segment by calendar effects.
answer_es: Compara vs periodos previos, usa baselines y segmenta por efectos de calendario.

question_en: How do you validate event tracking?
answer_en: Define schema, test end-to-end, compare client/server logs, monitor drops and duplicates.
answer_es: Define schema, prueba end-to-end, compara logs, monitorea caídas y duplicados.

question_en: What’s a safe rollout strategy?
answer_en: Start small (1%), monitor guardrails, ramp gradually, keep rollback plan.
answer_es: Empieza pequeño, monitorea guardrails, aumenta gradual y plan de rollback.

question_en: How do you evaluate a pricing change?
answer_en: Use experiments if possible; track revenue, conversion, churn, refunds, and long-term cohorts.
answer_es: Si se puede, experimento; mide revenue, conversión, churn, reembolsos y cohortes.

question_en: What are unit economics?
answer_en: Per-unit revenue and cost (LTV, CAC, margin) to assess sustainability.
answer_es: Ingresos y costos por unidad (LTV, CAC, margen) para evaluar sostenibilidad.

question_en: What’s an SRM and why does it matter?
question_es: ¿Qué es SRM y por qué importa?
answer_en: Sample ratio mismatch: allocation differs from expected; can indicate broken randomization.
answer_es: Sample ratio mismatch: la asignación difiere de lo esperado; puede indicar randomización rota.

question_en: How do you define an ‘active user’ metric?
answer_en: Tie it to meaningful value actions (not just opens), pick window, and keep consistent across reporting.
answer_es: Acciones de valor (no solo abrir), define ventana y mantén consistencia.

question_en: Primary metric up but guardrail down. Ship or not?
question_es: Sube métrica principal pero baja guardrail. ¿Lanzas o no?
answer_en: Quantify net value, validate significance/CI, analyze segments, mitigate harms; avoid broad ship if guardrail impact is meaningful.
answer_es: Cuantifica valor neto, valida IC, segmenta, mitiga daños; evita lanzamiento amplio si el daño es significativo.

question_en: How do you debug a metric with strong selection bias?
question_es: ¿Cómo depuras una métrica con sesgo de selección fuerte?
answer_en: Look for confounders, define comparable cohorts, use experiments/quasi-experiments, and avoid naive comparisons.
answer_es: Busca confusores, cohortes comparables, experimentos/cuasi-experimentos y evita comparaciones ingenuas.

question_en: How do you prevent metric gaming?
answer_en: Choose metrics tied to value, add guardrails, audit incentives, and monitor for distribution shifts/edge behaviors.
answer_es: Métricas ligadas a valor, guardrails, auditar incentivos y monitorear shifts/comportamientos.

question_en: Design an experiment when you can’t randomize.
question_es: Diseña un experimento cuando no puedes aleatorizar.
answer_en: Use quasi-experiments: DID, synthetic controls, regression discontinuity, or phased rollouts with careful assumptions.
answer_es: Usa cuasi-experimentos: DID, controles sintéticos, discontinuidad o rollouts por fases.

question_en: How do you evaluate long-term impact vs short-term lift?
question_es: ¿Cómo evalúas impacto a largo plazo vs lift corto plazo?
answer_en: Track cohorts over time, monitor guardrails, and use holdouts/long-run experiments when feasible.
answer_es: Sigue cohortes, monitorea guardrails y usa holdouts/experimentos largos si se puede.

question_en: What is the counterfactual in product experiments?
answer_en: What would have happened to the same users without the change; randomization approximates it.
answer_es: Lo que habría pasado sin el cambio; randomización lo aproxima.

question_en: What are common attribution pitfalls?
answer_en: Last-click bias, missing channels, selection bias, and ignoring incrementality; use experiments when possible.
answer_es: Sesgo last-click, canales faltantes, sesgo de selección e ignorar incrementalidad; usa experimentos si puedes.

question_en: How do you align metrics to OKRs without creating perverse incentives?
question_es: ¿Cómo alineas métricas a OKRs sin incentivos perversos?
answer_en: Use a small set of outcome metrics with guardrails, review regularly, and tie incentives to balanced scorecards.
answer_es: Pocas métricas outcome con guardrails, revisión frecuente e incentivos balanceados.
